column64: One observation I’ve had across different hospitals is that while many groups focus on creating efficient processes for their EHR users, their internal processes often don’t follow the same level of rigor or guidelines. Only one organization I’ve worked with applied the same level of attention to its internal workflows as it did to evaluating and implementing EHR workflows. This hospital had a notably more effective, clean, and workable EHR as a result. In contrast, the lack of focus on internal IT processes at other organizations I've seen has led to a more scattered system.   A possible survey question: 'Do you believe the same level of focus is given to internal efficiency/workflow processes as is to the EHR build and optimization? or something similar.

column41: We acquired another organization. We were told that the application would mimic our current facility structure.  This was not a clear expectation to the new facility, and they ended up customizing build to meet their wants; this has since put a strain on the workload as we are now rebuilding the system because they are not working as expected.  A clear understanding would be that workflows need to change to adapt to the functionality of the system, and if not, you are already unsuccessful when you try to use the same workflow in a new EHR.
